steps.title: What this function answers
steps.narrative:
Given a candidate replacement pGraph (right) and a current root pRoot (left, with MFFC labeled), return how many AIG ANDs would actually be added if we materialized the graph — taking strash-table reuse into account.

This is the number every commit-or-skip decision sits on top of: gain = MFFC size − count.

panes.title: Setup
panes.body: (1) Cut leaves' pFunc fields are pre-set to the AIG nodes that play those roles in the materialized graph. (2) The MFFC of pRoot is labeled with the current TravId (§MFFC).

steps.title: Setup: cut {a, c, d, n6}; candidate is 3 fanin-2 ANDs
steps.narrative:
From the §rewrite trace: cut at f is {a, c, d, n6}; MFFC of f w.r.t. that cut is {f, n8, n7, n9}. The candidate (right) implements the same function at f using three fanin-2 ANDs with edge negations (dashed = inverted edge):

m1 = ¬a · ¬c  m2 = d · ¬m1  m3 = ¬n6 · ¬m2

Output is m3. Algebraically m3 = ¬n6 · ¬(d·(a+c)) = ¬n6 · ¬(a·d) · ¬(c·d) — exactly the function the original f computes.

steps.title: Visit m1 = ¬a · ¬c — strash MISS → ADD
steps.narrative: Abc_AigAndLookup(¬a, ¬c). The current AIG has only n6=a·b, n7=a·d, n8=¬n6·¬n7, n9=c·d, f. None matches the (a-negated, c-negated) signature — miss. count = 1.

steps.title: Visit m2 = d · ¬m1 — m1 is brand-new → MISS → ADD
steps.narrative: Abc_AigAndLookup(d, ¬m1). m1 was just created in the previous step; nothing in the existing AIG references it. Lookup misses by definition. count = 2.

steps.title: Visit m3 = ¬n6 · ¬m2 — MISS → ADD
steps.narrative:
Abc_AigAndLookup(¬n6, ¬m2). m2 is also fresh; can't match anything. Miss → add. count = 3.

The structural check at decAbc.c:196..197 also asks: would this top-level AND equal pRoot? If yes, return -1 (self-loop). For our candidate, ¬n6·¬m2 isn't structurally f (f's fanin1 is ¬n9, not ¬m2 over a freshly-built cone), so the check passes.

steps.title: Return: nNodesAdded = 3 → gain = 4 − 3 = 1
steps.narrative: Final answer: 3 ANDs would be added. Combined with MFFC = 4, gain = 1 → accept (under default fUseZeros=0).

panes.title: When this function returns -1
panes.body: Two cases: (a) running count exceeds NodeMax = MFFC size — bail out early; (b) the new top-level node would equal pRoot structurally (a self-loop, decAbc.c:196..197). Both are short-circuits; not all candidates need full evaluation.

steps.title: Why MFFC tagging matters (hypothetical)
steps.narrative:
Our candidate happens to miss everywhere. But class 12 also has candidates whose intermediate ANDs do match existing structure — for instance one that computes t1 = a·d directly. Abc_AigAndLookup(a, d) would hit n7. Without further checks, rewrite would assume t1 reuses n7 for free — count = 0 for that step.

But n7 is in the MFFC: it's about to be deleted post-commit. The TravId check at decAbc.c:202 catches this: Abc_NodeIsTravIdCurrent(n7) → true → the hit is illusory → count as add anyway. Without the check we'd post-commit dangle a pointer to a deallocated node.

Real reuse only counts when the hit lands on a node outside the MFFC.

panes.body: Same logic governs refactor (abcRefactor.c:207) and the various candidate-scoring sites in resub. All three operators commit only if Dec_GraphToNetworkCount's return strictly beats their nMinSaved threshold.
